actionLibraryList.title: Seated 90/90 Stretch
actionLibraryList.context: Alternatively rotate your hips, keep your upper body upright, and maintain a 90-degree angle at your hips and knees.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Sit on the floor with your legs split. Bend one knee and bring it out to the side, creating a 90-degree angle at your hip and knee.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Alternatively roatate your hip, keep your upper body upright, always keep a 90-degree angle at your hip and knee.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Complete the full range of motion.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749718178177.jpg","context":"1.Rotate your hips alternately.\n2.Maintain a neutral spine position.\n3.Keep your hips and knees at a 90° angle throughout."}]
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Ensure a full range of motion.

actionLibraryList.title: Dual-Handle Overhead Squat
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand with feet shoulder-width apart or slightly wider, toes turned slightly outward. Engage your core.
2.Grip one handle in each hand and raise both arms straight overhead, locking out your elbows.
3.Inhale to prepare, then hinge at the hips and bend the knees to squat down until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly lower, keeping your arms locked overhead throughout.
4.Exhale and drive through your glutes and legs to extend the hips and knees, returning to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750238462550.jpg","context":"1.Stand with feet shoulder-width apart.\n2.Hold a handle in each hand, press them overhead with arms fully extended and locked."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750238466304.jpg","context":"1.Bend at the hips and knees, keeping your knees in line with your toes.\n2.Lower until your thighs are parallel or slightly below parallel to the ground."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Inhale as you lower, exhale as you rise.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid bending your arms or allowing the handles to shift forward or backward.

actionLibraryList.title: Single-Arm Romanian Deadlift
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Adjust the grips to ground level.
2.Keep your shoulders back, core engaged, and knees slightly bent.
3.Drive your hips backwards.
4.Avoid letting your knees bend too much or your upper body lose posture.
5.Continue bending and feel the stretch in your hamstrings.
6.Pause briefly at the bottom, then drive your hips forward and return to standing.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Attach the pulley block to the floor with one handle secured.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Keep your legs straight and hinge at the hips until your spine is parallel to the ground.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Then extend your hips to return to the standing position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Make sure your back is straight and your spine is extended.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Exhale as you rise and inhale as you lower.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Hold at the top with a strong glute squeeze. Drive your hips forward as you stand up.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Stand tall and hold the handle with one hand.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1751507489051.jpg"},{"context":"1.Hinge at your hips, keeping your back neutral.\n2.Slightly bend your knees and feel the stretch in your hamstrings.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1751507494100.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you rise and inhale as you lower.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Hold at the top with a strong glute squeeze. Drive your hips forward as you stand up.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Make sure your back is straight and your spine is extended.

actionLibraryList.title: Dual-Handle Calf Raise
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Adjust the grips to ground level.
2.Stand in a neutral position, holding the handle with both hands. Brace your core and keep your body stable.                                                                                                                                                       3.Perform a calf raise. Fully squeeze your calf muscles, and then return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Stand in a neutral position, holding the handle with both hands.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Rise up onto your toes, and fully squeeze the calf muscle group.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Exhale as you go up, inhale as you go down.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Keep your shoulders down and hold the handle with both hands.\n2.Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing forward.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748934516077.jpg"},{"context":"1.Perform a calf raise. Fully squeeze your calf muscles, and then return to the starting position.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748934520001.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale on the way up, inhale on the way down.

actionLibraryList.title: Dual-Handle Triceps Bench Press
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Lie on a flat bench, hold the handles above your chest with palms facing each other, elbows close to the body.
2.Engage your core and retract your shoulder blades.
3.Press the handles upward until nearly fully extended, keeping a slight bend in the elbows to avoid locking out.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748424348312.jpg","context":"1.Hands holding a narrow grip, arms fully extended in front of the chest."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748424351627.jpg","context":"1.Avoid fully locking out your elbows."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you press up, inhale as you lower back down.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep shoulders down, elbows tucked in, and avoid any body sway throughout the entire movement.

actionLibraryList.title: Seated Single-Leg Abduction
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Sit on the floor with your back straight and core engaged.
2.Secure the strap around your ankle, keeping your working leg under tension.
3.Keep a slight bend in your knee and move your leg out to the side to its full range.
4.Pause for 1–2 seconds and feel the contraction in your outer glute.
5.Slowly control your leg back to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750404079013.jpg","context":"1.Sit tall with your chest up and core engaged.\n2.Keep a slight bend in your knees."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750404110009.jpg","context":"1.Extend your working leg out to its full range."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you lift, inhale as you return.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your pelvis stable and avoid compensating with your lower back.

actionLibraryList.title: Alternating Hand to Elbow Plank
actionLibraryList.context: Start in a standard forearm plank with your core engaged and body in a straight line. One hand at a time, press into the ground, pushing up into a high plank, then lower back down to the forearm position, alternating sides. Keep your pelvis stable throughout, avoiding side-to-side movement or sagging of the lower back.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750756365657.jpg","context":"1.Engage your core, keeping your body in a straight line."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750756370157.jpg","context":"1.Place one hand on the ground at a time, pushing up into a high plank."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you push up, inhale as you return.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your pelvis stable, avoiding side-to-side motion or lower back sag.
